theme: The Leaderboard — Team-level Scoring, Escalation, and Integration Surface

shots.title: Multi-agent scorecard aggregation
shots.notes: AgentBreakdown type + agents field on GolfScorecard. buildAgentBreakdowns() computes per-agent score/stats. auto-card gains --swarm flag. Backward compatible. 6 new tests.

shots.title: Escalation rules
shots.notes: EscalationConfig with 3 triggers: blocker_timeout, claim_conflict, test_failure_cascade. detectEscalation() + buildEscalationEvent(). CLI slope escalate. Config under orchestration.escalation. 18 new tests.

shots.title: Team handicap + swarm performance
shots.notes: computeTeamHandicap() with per-role handicap, swarm efficiency, role combination analysis. CLI slope card --swarm. 15 new tests. One test fix for card shots count mismatch.

shots.title: Integration surface + MCP orchestration tests
shots.notes: Complete MCP registry for all orchestration primitives: standup, escalation, team handicap, agent aggregation. Type definitions. 10 new MCP integration tests (registry + sandbox execution).

conditions.description: Flow state continued from S14 — session continuation with full context of swarm primitives

nutrition.description: Full build+test+typecheck after every ticket (804 tests at completion)
Commit-per-ticket discipline — 4 commits for 4 tickets, pushed after each
49 new tests: 6 builder + 18 escalation + 15 team-handicap + 10 mcp-tools
One test fix in S15-3 (card shots vs agent shots mismatch) — caught inline. 6th consecutive clean sprint.

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Sixth consecutive par sprint. The leaderboard primitives landed cleanly — agent breakdown follows the same additive pattern (optional field, backward compatible). Escalation rules provide the missing safety net for swarm coordination.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: AgentBreakdown is display-only — it enriches the scorecard but doesn't change scoring logic. auto-card --swarm maps commits to sessions via branch matching. SwarmEfficiency's efficiency_ratio uses card.shots.length (total), not sum of agent shots. Team handicap per-role 'handicap' field is average score, not differential like the main handicap.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The test failure in S15-3 was subtle — the makeCard helper creates 4 default shots, but the agent only had 2 shots. The efficiency calculation uses card.shots.length, so 4/(4+2)=66.7%, not 2/(2+2)=50%. Fixed by aligning card shots with agent shots in the test.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: SLOPE now has the complete orchestration stack: roles, communication (standup), coordination (claims + escalation), and performance tracking (team handicap). The MCP surface is fully documented — Caddystack has a clear integration path.

yardage_book_updates: packages/core/src/types.ts: AgentBreakdown interface, agents field on GolfScorecard
packages/core/src/builder.ts: buildAgentBreakdowns(), AgentShotInput, agents passthrough in buildScorecard()
packages/core/src/escalation.ts: EscalationConfig, detectEscalation(), buildEscalationEvent(), 3 trigger types
packages/core/src/team-handicap.ts: computeTeamHandicap(), per-role handicap, swarm efficiency, role combinations
packages/cli/src/commands/auto-card.ts: --swarm flag, buildSwarmAgents() maps commits to sessions
packages/cli/src/commands/escalate.ts: slope escalate CLI (manual + auto-detect modes)
packages/mcp-tools/src/registry.ts: 12 new entries (standup, escalation, team-handicap, agent breakdown)
course_management_notes: 4 tickets, 4 in_the_hole — zero misses, zero hazards (6th consecutive clean sprint)
Tests grew from 755 to 804 (+49 across core, mcp-tools)
Cross-package changes: core types/builder/config/index, cli auto-card/card/index, mcp-tools registry/tests
